key_insight: V-alloying MoTe2 to form Mo0.75V0.25Te2 breaks valley degeneracy and enables large, tunable valley polarization that can be further boosted by electric field and biaxial strain.
engineering_relevance: This makes Mo0.75V0.25Te2 a promising 2D candidate for low-power valleytronic devices such as transistors and sensors.
method: Performed first-principles DFT calculations on V-alloyed MoTe2 monolayer.
Included spin-orbit coupling and magnetic exchange effects to assess valley splitting.
Checked structural and dynamical stability via energetics and phonon spectra.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports stable phonon behavior with no imaginary modes and quantifies valley splitting up to 132.8 meV under electric field and 160.8 meV under tensile strain.
watch_item: Results are computational and depend on alloy composition, strain control, and field alignment, so experimental realization and defect effects may alter the predicted valley splitting.
paper_title: Strain- and Electric-Field-Tunable Valley Polarization in Mo0.75V0.25Te2(Mo3VTe8) for Valleytronic Application
